commit.message:
feat: add links command — graph health analysis (#46)

New command: npx @anokye-labs/kbexplorer links

Analyzes the knowledge graph and reports:
- Orphan nodes (zero connections)
- Broken references (connections to non-existent nodes)
- Weak clusters (no cross-cluster edges)
- Missing cross-references (content mentions without edges)
- Coverage gaps (source files with no content node)

Tested against kbexplorer-template: found 27 orphans, 179 missing
cross-references, 0 broken refs, 0 weak clusters.

Supports --json flag for programmatic output.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: implement VideoStore interface and SqliteVideoStore (#78) (#83)

- Implement SqliteVideoStore class with full SQL logic and tag serialization
- Add comprehensive tests using openDb(':memory:') + initializeSchema(db)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: ZSH completion for kubectl unbounded-net (#31)

* fix: rebuild images with proper version tag in release workflow

Replace imagetools re-tagging with actual Docker builds using the
release version tag. The CI layer cache means only the final VERSION
layer changes (~1s per image). Also builds plugins with the release
version ldflags instead of downloading from the CI run (which had
dev versions).

The release workflow now has:
- version: detect release merge, extract version from CHANGELOG
- build (matrix): rebuild images per-arch with proper VERSION arg
- manifest: create multi-arch manifests from per-arch images
- release: tag, render manifests, build plugins, SBOM, draft release

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: ZSH completion for kubectl unbounded-net

The completion script referenced the old 'unbounded cni' command name,
causing completions to fail in ZSH. Updated:
- Root command Use: 'unbounded cni' -> 'unbounded-net'
- ZSH compdef targets to match 'unbounded-net'
- requestComp to use 'kubectl unbounded-net' for subcommand dispatch
- Usage display helpers to use 'kubectl unbounded-net'

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: hardcode GOEXPERIMENT=nosystemcrypto in CI plugin builds

The 'go version | grep microsoft' detection doesn't work because
Microsoft Go's version string doesn't contain 'microsoft'. CI always
uses Microsoft Go (via go-download-base-url), so hardcode the
experiment in workflows. Makefile detection updated to check for the
systemcrypto_nocgo_linux.go file in GOROOT instead.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: rename Build/Test workflow to CI

Also update the release.yml workflow_run trigger to match.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: auto-remove legacy flowsmartly-apps.conf on nginx regenerate

The old V2 nginx-config wrote location blocks to conf.d/flowsmartly-apps.conf
which is invalid (location directives require server context). V3 now writes
to flowsmartly-upstreams.conf + flowsmartly-locations/ dir. This cleanup
ensures the legacy file is deleted whenever regenerateAndReload() runs.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix nullable description in ModelSummary causing empty models list

Backend always returns description: null but Zod schema used
z.string().optional() which rejects null values, throwing at parse
time and leaving availableModels as [].

- contracts: mark ModelSummary.description as nullable: true
- frontend schemas: z.string().optional() → z.string().nullish()
- frontend types: description?: string → description?: string | null

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(formatter): semicolons on own line, AS alignment, graceful parse errors (#1)

> [!NOTE]
> This PR description was authored by GitHub Copilot CLI (Claude Sonnet
4.6) on behalf of @johnallen3d.

## Summary

Three formatter style fixes from real-world usage feedback.

### Trailing semicolons on their own line
Previously `;` was appended to the last line of each statement. Now it
always appears as a standalone line.

### Column alias AS alignment
When a SELECT has 2+ aliased columns (all single-line), the `AS`
keywords are padded to align vertically:

```sql
SELECT
   first_name    AS first
  ,last_name     AS last
  ,email_address AS email
FROM t
```

Multi-line column expressions (e.g. `CASE`) disable alignment for the
whole SELECT.

### Graceful handling of parse failures
`format_sql` now returns `(formatted_sql, warnings)` instead of
crashing. On `ParseError`, the original SQL is returned unchanged with a
warning. This partially addresses #2 (`PIVOT ... USING ... ORDER BY ...`
not yet supported by sqlglot) — the formatter no longer crashes; a
proper fix is tracked separately.

## Changes

- `formatter.py` — returns `(str, list[FormatWarning])` tuple; catches
`ParseError` gracefully
- `generator.py` — adds `alias_sql` override with `_as_align_width`
state for AS alignment
- `cli.py` — unpacks the new tuple, prints warnings as `WARN` instead of
crashing
- All callers updated to unpack the tuple
- 4 new tests; 44/44 passing
- `tmp/` added to `.gitignore`

Resolves #2

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: meal plan GET auto-computes week start from any given date

The dashboard widget passed today's date as the week param, but the
API used it as-is for week_start lookup. Meal plans are stored by
week start (e.g. 2026-04-06), not by today's date (2026-04-10).

Now the GET handler fetches the family's weekStartDay from settings
and computes the correct week_start from any date in that week,
so the widget (and any caller) no longer needs to pre-compute it.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add admin PUT rebuild endpoint for store redeploy

PUT /api/admin/migrate-stores-v3?storeId=xxx triggers buildStoreV3+deployStoreV3
without re-running the AI agent (cheaper, for deploy-failed stores).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add Content Editor: AI-powered suggestions, drafts, and content plans

New 'Content Editor' view in the sidebar with three capabilities:

1. **New Content suggestions** — identifies uncovered documentation topics
   grouped by category, with priority badges and topic counts
2. **Update recommendations** — flags partially-matched modules that need
   content expansion, showing match scores
3. **Outdated module detection** — surfaces modules >12 months old that
   need refreshing

Each suggestion has a 'Generate Draft' button that uses GitHub Models AI to
produce:
- For new modules: complete index.yml draft with title, summary, learning
  objectives, units, prerequisites, and suggested repo placement
- For updates: specific content changes, new units, and revised objectives
- For outdated: staleness assessment, rewrite plan, and refresh checklist

Additional features:
- 'Content Plan' button generates a full prioritized plan (immediate /
  short-term / long-term) exportable as Markdown
- Draft preview panel with markdown rendering
- Export drafts as YAML, Markdown, or JSON
- Copy to clipboard support
- Auto-builds suggestions when navigating to editor view

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(generation): normalize keyTakeaways and comparisonTable from free model responses

Free models may return keyTakeaways as a string or non-array, causing
'.map is not a function' crash at step 9/12.

- Extend normalizeKeyTakeawaysArray() to handle string (split by newline/comma)
  and non-array types (set to undefined) in addition to arrays
- Call normalizeKeyTakeawaysArray() in pre-5c normalization block
- Guard comparisonTable.rows as array

- location blocks written to /etc/nginx/flowsmartly-locations/ (server context)
- main flowsmartly server block must include /etc/nginx/flowsmartly-locations/*.conf
- fixes 'location directive not allowed here' nginx error

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: sub-agent tabs now open via task tool detection

The SDK doesn't emit subagent.started/subagent.completed events.
Sub-agents are launched via the 'task' tool, so we now:
- Detect task tool in onPreToolUse/onPostToolUse hooks
- Extract agent name/description from tool args
- Emit subagent_start/subagent_complete from there
- Auto-create tab as fallback if delta arrives with parentToolCallId
  but no tab exists yet

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[RCCL] Fix netOverride being skipped when rail trees are enabled (restore desired NIC mapping for targeted 4-NIC systems) (#4726)

## Motivation

This PR resolves a performance issue where enabling rail-optimized trees
caused an early return, skipping the netOverride logic on affected
systems.

On some 4-NIC MI3xx systems, this manifested as:

- enabling rail trees appearing to regress ring performance
- inconsistent behavior depending on whether rail trees were selected

`netOverride` is required for pairing GPUs with no PXB path to NICs on
these systems.

## Technical Details

1. Remove early return after treeRail success
2. Refactor override into helper `applyNetOverride(system,
romeTopoModels[i].options);`
3. Apply override after graph construction
4. Now runs regardless of what ring/tree are matched
5. Add runtime control `RCCL_DISABLE_NET_OVERRIDE` for debugging issues
with netOverride due to hard assumptions about system layout

## JIRA ID
ROCM-3200

## Test Plan

<!-- Explain any relevant testing done to verify this PR. -->

## Test Result

<!-- Briefly summarize test outcomes. -->

## Submission Checklist

- [ ] Look over the contributing guidelines at
https://github.com/ROCm/ROCm/blob/develop/CONTRIBUTING.md#pull-requests.

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: Epic 5 — Active Workout Page redesign

E5-T1: Sticky timer topbar — breadcrumb (← Workouts), large monospace
  accent-coloured timer centred, Complete button pinned right.
  Sits below the mobile fixed topbar via top: var(--topbar-height);
  drops to top: 0 on desktop (≥768 px). Backdrop blur for glass effect.

E5-T2: Redesign exercise cards — dark .card surface, exercise name
  prominent with sets-count badge; set rows replace the <table>:
  each row shows set #, weight (kg), reps, and icon buttons.

E5-T3: Animate set rows — TransitionGroup name=list for slide-in on
  add; done sets get accent-filled circle checkmark + faded row
  background (no strikethrough).

E5-T4: Styled exercise select — native <select> with .input .select
  classes (custom chevron, appearance: none) inside a flex row.

E5-T5: Per-exercise collapse toggle — collapsedExercises Set<number>
  ref; chevron icon rotates -90 deg when collapsed; Transition
  name=collapse wraps the sets wrapper.

E5-T6: Inline confirms replace confirm() dialogs —
  confirmComplete (topbar) toggles between Complete button and a
  Finish? / Yes / No row (Transition name=fade mode=out-in);
  confirmRemoveId tracks which exercise shows Remove? / Yes / No.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add sparse matrix transpose teaching visualizer (part3)

Three static files for an interactive step-by-step algorithm visualizer:

- sparse_matrix_visualizer.html  — page structure (Chinese UI, 6 sections)
- sparse_matrix_visualizer.css   — clean light theme with colored highlights
- sparse_matrix_visualizer.js    — complete step generation + playback engine

Features:
* Visualizes all three C algorithms from part3/sparse_matrix.c:
  transpose_naive (O(cols×terms)), transpose_fast (O(cols+terms)),
  transpose_fast_optimized (O(cols+terms), single aux array)
* Steps generated fully upfront; play/pause/prev/next/reset + speed control
* 2×2 data grid: source 6×6 matrix, source triple table,
  result triple table (9-slot fixed), result 6×6 matrix
* Per-step highlights: scanned column, matched triple, written slot,
  num[]/cpot[] cell being updated
* Algorithm state panel shows live num[], cpot[], naive loop vars
* Complexity comparison table with speedup estimates

All three algorithms verified to produce identical correct result:
  (1,3,-3)(1,5,15)(2,1,12)(3,1,9)(3,4,24)(3,6,-4)(4,5,-7)(5,4,18)(6,3,14)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(generation): coerce array content fields from free models before quality validation

OpenRouter free models sometimes return section.content and faq.answer as arrays
instead of strings. This causes 's.content?.trim is not a function' crash at step 5c.

- Add pre-5c normalizer that joins arrays to newline-separated strings
- Make validateArticleOutputQuality() safe to non-string content/answer fields

commit.message:
[development-pm-c361] Complete TASK-184: Launch planning strategy

Created comprehensive go-to-market launch plan for Phase 2 visual canvas release:

Launch Channels:
- Product Hunt (page setup, hunter outreach, launch day schedule)
- Hacker News (timing strategy, post format, engagement plan)
- Social media (Twitter/X 10-tweet thread, LinkedIn, Reddit 5 subreddits)
- Email outreach (warm network, TypeScript influencers, cold leads)
- Community (Discord, GitHub, developer relations)
- Press (dev/AI publications, press release template)

Deliverables:
- Master timeline (pre-launch week, launch day, 4-week growth)
- Content templates (PH First Comment, HN post, email templates)
- Success metrics (Week 1: 500+ stars, 200+ downloads; Week 4: 2K+ stars)
- Risk mitigation (technical issues, negative feedback, competitive response)
- Launch checklist (40+ action items across product, marketing, community)

Target: Launch Tuesday 12:01am PST after TASK-185 QA completion.

commit.message:
Add structured logging to ConversationGrain and OllamaHttpClient

ConversationGrain:
- Log conversation initialization (new and already-initialized)
- Log model overrides on send
- Log Ollama request (model, message count) and response latency
- Log errors with context before re-throwing
- Log GetHistory on uninitialized grain

OllamaHttpClient:
- Log outgoing requests (model, message count, base address)
- Log Ollama response sizes and model counts
- Log warnings on HTTP errors and connection failures

Implement core PinePal features

- BLE device discovery and connection with exponential backoff reconnection

- Desktop notification forwarding to watch via D-Bus monitoring

- Flatpak manifest with Bluetooth and D-Bus permissions

Based on BLE protocol from watchmate by Andrii Zymohliad.

repository.description: Extends on https://github.com/FaultyFunctions/SoulGraves with a variety of useful features

commit.message:
feat: per-model passthrough routing via PASSTHROUGH_MODELS

Replace global PROXY_MODE with PASSTHROUGH_MODELS config. Routes are
now decided per-request based on model prefix matching:
- Models matching configured prefixes → Anthropic passthrough (/messages)
- All other models → OpenAI conversion (/chat/completions)

This allows a single proxy to serve mixed backends (e.g., GLM via
OpenAI format + MiniMax via Anthropic format on the same endpoint).

Example: PASSTHROUGH_MODELS="minimax" routes minimax-* models to
Anthropic passthrough while glm-* models go through OpenAI conversion.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: wp-document-revisions
repository.description: A document management and version control plugin that allows teams of any size to collaboratively edit files and manage their workflow.

commit.message:
feat(typescript-development): add fnm as recommended Node.js version manager

Add fnm (Fast Node Manager) as the recommended tool for Node.js version
management. fnm is Rust-based, cross-platform, and reads .node-version
files — the Node.js analog to pyenv reading .python-version.

Changes:
- SKILL.md: add fnm to assumptions, annotate .node-version with consumer
- references/standards.md: new Runtime Version Management section with
  setup instructions (PowerShell + Bash), project integration, and
  three-mechanism pinning table (fnm + engines + Corepack)
- INTENT.md: document fnm decision and rationale vs Volta/nvm
- skill-cli-development/SKILL.md: add execution model section, clarify
  run-from-source requirement

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Polish edit button, fix swap teams, rename delete label

1. Edit pen: full opacity, small square button right-aligned in the
   same row as match result (flex layout)
2. Swap Teams no longer swaps the score — only players move
3. Delete button text: 'Mark as Deleted' → 'Delete Match'

Two-agent repeated Prisoner's Dilemma environment for MARL experiments
with partner selection capabilities.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: nav scroll persistence + NetworkMemberships null guards

Navigation:
- Add useRef on .nav-menu scroll container
- Restore scroll position from sessionStorage on mount
- Save scroll position debounced via rAF on scroll event
- Scroll active item into view only when outside visible area (avoids jump on every render)

NetworkMemberships:
- Defensive array extraction: try result.data fallback if result is not an array
- Filter out null/undefined items after fetch
- Optional chaining on e?.enrollmentStatus in filter calls
- Index-based fallback keys (e?.id ?? 'pending-N') in all map renders

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix(checkin): defer auto-checkin re-evaluation

Add a deferred auto-checkin check in the coordinator
update path for awaiting_checkin state.  When the
monitoring switch was not loaded during restore and the
fallback conservatively blocked auto-checkin, the next
coordinator update re-evaluates once the switch is
available.  This prevents the sensor from getting stuck
in awaiting_checkin when monitoring is actually off.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Grimberg <tykeal@bardicgrove.org>

commit.message:
Add debug logging and improve error handling in stream fetcher

Use Promise.allSettled to handle individual channel failures gracefully
and add console logging to track stream fetching and serving.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: sub-agent activity tabs with live streaming output

When a sub-agent runs, a 🤖 tab opens showing real-time reasoning,
tool calls, and progress. Deltas with parentToolCallId are routed
to the agent tab instead of main chat. Tab stays open for review
after completion.

Feature-flagged: localStorage.setItem('dg-agent-tabs', '1') to enable.
Disabled by default — falls back to existing system messages.
